text.title: growth-podcast-strategy Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for growth-podcast-strategy. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Default to guest appearances before hosting a show; guest strategy delivers faster ROI with zero equipment investment.
rule.rationale: Guest appearances reach existing audiences immediately. A single niche podcast appearance can produce 150+ trial signups and 30 paying customers. Hosting requires 12-24 months to deliver equivalent authority.

rule.statement: Target podcasts with 50K-500K listeners in your niche; do not pitch 1M+ listener shows as a first step.
rule.rationale: Large shows require established credentials and relationships. Mid-tier niche shows have higher booking rates and more targeted audiences for B2B products.

rule.statement: Reference one specific recent episode by name in every pitch; a pitch without a named episode signals templating and is ignored.
rule.rationale: Personalization is the primary differentiator; hosts receive dozens of generic pitches weekly.

rule.statement: Lead with audience value, not the guest's credentials; propose 3 specific bullet points the episode will cover.
rule.rationale: Hosts prioritize audience benefit over guest status; credential-first pitches convert at one-third the rate of audience-value pitches.

rule.statement: Record 3 episodes before publishing any of them; launching without a backlog causes schedule failures that permanently damage algorithm placement.
rule.rationale: Inconsistency is the single biggest cause of podcast failure. A 3-episode buffer ensures week-1 and week-2 can publish on schedule even if week-3 recording fails.

rule.statement: Use a cold open (30-sec teaser from the episode) before the intro music; it hooks listeners before the generic intro can lose them.
rule.rationale: Podcast platforms show 30-second previews; a cold open converts at 2x the rate of a music-first intro.

rule.statement: Research the last 5 interviews of every guest before preparing questions; generic biography openers waste the opening 5 minutes.
rule.rationale: Guests repeat the same origin story on every show; starting with insight-first questions produces more valuable content and higher completion rates.

rule.statement: Feed agent a transcript after recording to generate show notes, chapter timestamps, tweet thread, and newsletter blurb in one pass.
rule.rationale: Post-production content generation from transcript is the highest-leverage agentic workflow for podcasting; it converts 60 minutes of recording into 5 distribution assets.
